mechanism_narrative: Near-zero federal funds rates reduce discount rates for equity valuations, driving positive forward returns as long as rates remain at the effective lower bound.
failure_condition: SPX 20d returns are negative in >55% of months when FEDFUNDS < 0.25%, or Rank IC < 0.02 over rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly decline in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.2 percentage points signals accelerating labor market recovery, which increases corporate earnings expectations and generates positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Forward 60-day returns are negative in >60% of instances where UNRATE drops >0.2pp, or the signal shows no statistical edge (p > 0.10) in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and industrial production indicates synchronized domestic demand recovery, which directly boosts corporate revenues and drives positive equity returns over the following month.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict positive 20d returns when RSAFS and INDPRO both rise, or interaction term coefficient is insignificant in multivariate regression over 5-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: In a liquidity-constrained environment, a monthly increase in the index of industrial production above 0.5% reflects capacity utilization improvements that trigger short-term momentum buying in equities.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are negative in >50% of months where INDPRO growth exceeds 0.5%, or the signal reverses direction during high-volatility regimes (VIX > 25).

mechanism_narrative: When core inflation remains below 2.0% while the federal funds rate stays at the effective lower bound, the central bank maintains accommodative policy, preventing discount rate spikes and supporting positive equity returns.
failure_condition: SPX delivers negative 20d returns in >55% of months meeting the trigger, or inflation unexpectedly accelerates above 2.5% without rate hikes, breaking the low-rate support mechanism.

mechanism_narrative: Rising total nonfarm payrolls directly correlate with expanding consumer spending capacity, which lifts corporate earnings forecasts and produces positive S&P 500 returns over the subsequent quarter.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns are negative in >60% of months with positive PAYEMS growth, or the correlation between payroll growth and equity returns falls below 0.10 in rolling 3-year samples.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly housing starts figure exceeding 550,000 units signals a stabilization in the residential construction sector, reducing systemic financial risk and triggering positive equity market reactions over the next month.
failure_condition: SPX 20d returns are negative in >55% of months where HOUST exceeds 550, or housing data revisions erase the initial signal predictive power within 30 days.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in real GDP and retail sales confirms broad-based economic expansion, which sustains corporate profit margins and generates positive S&P 500 returns over the following quarter.
failure_condition: The joint signal predicts negative 60d returns in >50% of quarters, or the interaction effect vanishes when GDP growth falls below 1.0% annualized.
